title: Proclamation No. 218, s. 1964
text:
MALACAÑANG

RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 218

RESERVING FOR MILITARY PURPOSES A CERTAIN PARCEL OF LAND OF THE PRIVATE DOMAIN SEATED IN THE BARRIOS OF SIBUL AND TARTARO, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MIGUEL, PROVINCE OF BULACAN, ISLAND OF LUZON

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources and pursuant to the authority vested in me by law, I, Diosdado Macapagal, President of the Philippines, do hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for military purposes under the administration of the Chief of Staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines to private rights, if any there be, a certain parcel of land of the private domain situated in the barrios of Sibul and Tartaro, Municipality of San Miguel, Province of Bulacan, Island of Luzon, and more particularly desbribed as follows:

Lot 2820-A Bsd-5208 (Military Training Camp Site)

A Parcel of land (Lot 2820-A of the subdivision plan Bsd-5208, being a portion of Lot 2820 of the cadastral survey of San Miguel, L.R.C. Cad. Record No.—–), situated in the Barrios of Sibu, and Tartaro, Municipality of San Miguel, Province of Bulacan. Bounded on the S., SW., N. and NW., along lines 1-2-3-4-5-6-7, by Lot 2720-B of the subdivision plan; on the E., along lines 7-8, by River; on the SE., S., and E., along lines 8-9-10-11-12-13-14-15, by Lot 2820-C of the subdivision plan; on the S., along line 15-16, by Provincial Road; and on the W. and S., along lines 16-17-1, by Lot 2820-B of the subdivision plan. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 41 o 36′ W., 723.51 meters from B.B.M. 34, San Miguel Cad., thence N. 89 o 59′ W., 323.94 meters to point 2; thence N. 2726′ W., 469.52 meters to point 3; thence E A S T 475.00 meters to point 4; thence S. 89 o 58′ E., 382.51 meters to point 5; thence N. 69 o 42′ E., 400.00 meters to point 6; thence N. 69 o 41′ E., 305.65 meters to point 7; thence S. 16 o 42′ W., 322.14 meters to point 8; thence S. 64 o 53′ W., 105.55 meters to point 9; thence N. 23 o 39′ W., 168.94 meters to point 10; thence S. 30 o 48′ W., 392.07 meters to point 11; thence S. 55 o 55′ W., 183.28 meters to point 12; thence S. 86 o 09′ W., 351.46 meters to point 13; thence S.0 o 02′ W., 463.78 meters to point 14; thence S. 0 o 02′ W., 0.92 meters to point 15; thence S. 80 o 35′ W., 20.00 meters to point 16; thence N. 0 o 02′ W., 1.32 meters to point 17; thence N. 0 o 02′ W., 466.73 meters to point of beginning; containing an area of 499, 759 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and the rest by B.L. Conc. Mons.; bearing true; date of the original survey, Jan. 1923—Jan., 1925 and that of the subdivision survey, Aug. 26-29, 1936 which was approved on Oct. 19, 1936.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 19th day of March, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-four.

DIOSDADO MACAPAGAL President of the Philippines

By the President: CALIXTO O. ZALDIVAR Acting Executive Secretary

Source : Legislative Library, House of Representatives
